Incorrect Investment Prognoses Through Analytical Software,"Greetings Customer Support, I am contacting you to address an issue we have encountered with the analytical tools at our disposal. The investment forecasts we have been obtaining have been inaccurate. We believe this problem could be due to issues with inconsistent data feed integration. We have attempted to solve this by restarting the system and reviewing the data source connections, but the issue remains unresolved. We would be grateful if you could investigate this matter and provide a solution at your earliest convenience. Please inform us if there is any additional information you require from us to resolve this issue. Thank you for your attention and cooperation.",0,1

SaaS Project Management Tool Experienced Unforeseen Malfunction Today,"Dear Support Team, I am contacting you to report an anomaly that occurred with our SaaS project management application. The interface stopped rendering data, and errors were displayed abruptly. We believe the cause might be rooted in a connection issue with the SQL Server 2019 database. Despite restarting the server and reviewing our browser add-ons, the problem remains unresolved. We would be grateful if you could examine this and propose a resolution promptly. Please inform us if further details are required from us. We look forward to your prompt response. Thanks for your attention and help. Best regards, [Your Name]",0,1

Re: Strange headers on mail from SA users list,">>I'd really like this to be fixed as its littering my mail archiving >>system with junk addresses. I've attached the complete, unmodified >>message as ""annoyance'txt"". > The message received at your end shows up as > ""Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit"" whereas the original message is > ""Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit"". > The transfer-encoding conversion may have caused the header problem. That sounds likely. Thanks for the information. I already have a filter installed immediately downstream of SA, so this is easy to check by extending it: any header byte containing 0x80 or higher is invalid because headers must be US-ASCII encoding (RFC 2821). I can log the details of any messages containing them. As for detoxifying the headers and stopping any further upsets, at a guess something like: if (c > 0x7f) { c = c & 0x80
column3: } would detoxify the invalid header bytes headers without adding significant problems. Comments? Martin",0,1

Re: URGENT: Our application is experiencing frequent API timeouts,"Dear John,

We have identified the issue causing the API timeouts. It was due to a surge in traffic that overloaded one of our database servers. We have implemented a fix and can confirm that the API endpoints are now responding normally.
We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this may have caused.
Please let us know if you experience any further issues.
Re: High API Latency and Errors - Urgent Assistance Needed,"Dear John,
Thank you for contacting Aetheros Support. We understand the urgency of this situation and apologize for the inconvenience caused.
We are currently investigating the reported API latency and errors. Our team is actively working to identify the root cause and implement a resolution.
To assist us in our investigation, could you please provide the following information:
* A list of the specific APIs experiencing issues
* The approximate timeframe when these issues began
* Any recent changes or updates made to your API configuration
We will keep you updated on our progress and provide further assistance as necessary.

column1: Off the Shelf - February 8, 2008,"Off the Shelf -- February 8, 2008 Special thanks to this week's sponsor: Verio Verio Is Linux with a Lineage Our Dedicated Servers deliver SAS70 Certification for critical data security and scalability, a Tier-1 Global IP network and premier data centers, and a dedicated, certified support team. From Verio, an industry leader with more than 500,000 business customers. Make the move at http://www.verio.com/ljnews2. Welcome to the February 8th edition of Off the Shelf.ГӮ Unfortunately, this is the final edition of our beloved newsletter. We hope that you have enjoyed it, and it has been our pleasure to bring it to you. Here is what you'll find inside this edition: Products and Projects [01] Paragent 3.0 (upgrade) [02] Linspire's Custom OS Build Service (new service) [03] PostgreSQL 8.3 (upgrade) [04] Permabit Enterprise Archive Data Center Series (new product line) The Bookshelf [05] Up and Running With Joomla by Skip Matheny & R. Allen Wyke (O'Reilly) [06] C++ GUI Programming With Qt4, 2nd Ed. by Jasmin Blanchette & Mark Summerfield (Prentice-Hall) Hoping you've enjoyed Off the Shelf...over and out, James Gray and the Linux Journal Editorial Team mfgkl@linuxjournal.com PRODUCTS AND PROJECTS [01] Paragent 3.0 (upgrade) If your upstart is too small to have its own IT administrator, Paragent has a product for you. Paragent 3.0 is an open-source, SaaS-based IT management application that provides real-time, on-demand data and information about a company's networked computers. The new release adds automatic software and inventory collection, advanced search and remote desktop functionality. Paragent claims to be one of the few SaaS companies pursuing an open source strategy. http://www.paragent.com [02] Linspire's Custom OS Build Service (new service) Our friends at Linspire continue to find new niches, the latest being a custom desktop Linux OS Build Service for its partners. The new custom-build service is designed to help those partners not only to get the exact feature sets they want but also cut their time to market and reduce the expense involved in building a desktop Linux operating system. One can build a Debian or Ubuntu-based desktop Linux OS with customizable software stacks and other features. http://www.linspire.com [03] PostgreSQL 8.3 (update) The PostgreSQL team is beamingly proud of the new Version 8.3 of the popular high-performance, object-relational database management system. Version 8.3 is said to provide greater consistency of performance than previous editions, ensuring that every user can depend on the same high performance demonstrated in recent benchmarks for every transaction. Other enhancements include over 280 patches, Heap Organized Tuples, spread checkpoints, background writer autotuning and asynchronous commit option - all of which accelerate transaction processing throughput between 5-30%. Many new features are included for application developers, as well. PostgreSQL is available for download from the organization's Web site.http://www.postgresql.org [04] Permabit Enterprise Archive Data Center SeriesГӮ (new product line) Heads up storage hounds, Permabit recently released Permabit Enterprise Archive Data Center Series, a line of scalable data-reduced storage solutions for enterprise archiving. Permabit's proposition is that 60-80% of data in the data center is static and sits on expensive, transaction-optimized primary storage. Their solution is to move large volumes of static data to more cost-effective tiers with the same level of assurance as primary storage. The solutions scale up to 3 PB and offer costs ranging from $0.33 to $2.33 per GB of storage, vs. $25 to 75 per GB for competing archiving solutions, claims Permabit. Permabit also says that its new RAIN-EC data protection technology is 250 times safer than RAID 6. http://www.permabit.com WHAT A SHAMELESS COMMERCIAL PLUG! Yes, the Web rocks, and LinuxJournal.com is getting better all the time. But there's nothing like sneaking away from the computer for awhile and curling up on the couch with a blanket, hot cocoa and the latest issue of Linux Journal. Experience the joy of Linux with all of your senses - turning the crisp pages and soaking up the vividly-designed layout. Go to http://www.linuxjournal.com/subscriptions to subscribe for as low as $2.07 per issue. THE BOOKSHELF [05] Up and Running With Joomla by Skip Matheny & R. Allen Wyke (O'Reilly) Up and Running With Joomla is O'Reilly's new Short Cut (brief PDF document) on getting your Web team rolling in the same direction when implementing the Joomla open-source CMS. The resource shows how to install, configure, customize, and extend a Joomla installation. Up and Running also offers a plethora of tricks and tips for managing both customers and content authors. http://www.oreilly.com [06] C++ GUI Programming With Qt4, 2nd Ed. by Jasmin Blanchette & Mark Summerfield (Prentice-Hall) Prentice-Hall says that C++ GUI Programming With Qt4 by Jasmin Blanchette & Mark Summerfield, now in its second edition, is the book that Trolltech uses to teach Qt to its own hires. Essentially two Trolltech insiders have written a start-to-finish guide to getting results with Qt 4.3 so you can build industrial-strength C++ applications that run natively on Windows, Linux/Unix, Mac OS X, and embedded Linux without source code changes. The book's approach is to use realistic examples and in-depth advice, illustrating the best Qt programming patterns for nearly every GUI development task, providing database access, integrating XML, using subclassing, composition, and more. http://www.informit.com FEATURED EVENTS Optimize and Explore the Business and Technology of Social PlatformsThe O'Reilly Graphing Social Patterns Conference (GSP) takes place March 3-6, and is collocated with the OГўвӮ¬в„ўReilly Emerging Technology Conference in San Diego, CA. GSP explores the components of building and distributing applications for networking platforms. Designed for business and marketing executives, as well as technical developers, GSP will give you the tools and know-how you need to make the most of your social networks. Use code gspw08ljra to save 10%! http://conferences.oreilly.com/graphingsocial. Open Source Seminars at IT360 Conference and Expo! Join us April 7 - 9, 2008 in Toronto at the leading IT event in Canada. Over three days of 90+ sessions and an immersive trade show floor, IT360 is cultivating innovation, so stay ahead of the curve and get on board today ~ save $$$ by registering early. http://www.it360.ca Attend InfoWorldГўвӮ¬в„ўs OSBC Event in San Francisco March 25-26, 2008InfoWorldГўвӮ¬в„ўs Open Source Business Conference (OSBC) brings together the top players in the venture eco-system, legal experts, CIOs, and IT professionals for two days to collaborate on emerging business models, strategies and profitability for open source software use. Seating is limited, register today at http://www.OSBC.com. -- If you do not want to receive this newsletter in the future, click this link. To update your preferences and to unsubscribe visit this link. Forward this masterpiece to a like-minded, enlightened soul by clicking this link.-- powered by phplist v 2.10.4, ГӮВ© tincan ltd",0,1
My reflections of the Outfoxed podcast with Adam Goldfarb,"How do you get a 20% positive reply rate from cold email in 2026?
The unobvious answer is: you stop acting like a cold emailer.

In Outfoxed episode 5, we spoke with Adam Goldfarb about what effective cold email looks like in 2026. I recently published a breakdown explaining how to apply his approach in practical experiments to improve reply rates.
- How to test whether your email feels like spam using the ""Nuisance Test""
- Why 80% of your time should go into research and 20% into writing
- How to lead with credibility in your opening sentence
- Why shorter emails (3вҖ“4 sentences) often perform better
If you're rebuilding email sequences for 2026, this article outlines practical steps you can test immediately.

HereвҖҷs the latest on CanadiansвҖҷ debt problems, international students facing deportation and OntarioвҖҷs new energy conservation initiative. DONвҖҷT MISS Canadian Press/Andrew Vaughan economy Canadians are hurting as their debt problems mount вҖ” and now the banks are suffering too At the start of the pandemic, interest rates were low, financial support was flowing and banks werenвҖҷt aggressively collecting debts. вҖңIt was really masking the fact that people were using credit to make ends meet,вҖқ one expert said. But now, collections and interest rates are up, he said, and the situation no longer seems manageable. A series of reports this week put it in black and white: Canada has the highest debt-to-GDP ratio in the G7, and credit and car loan delinquencies are increasing. Now, CanadaвҖҷs Big Five banks have taken a hit, too, as they reported putting aside billions of dollars to protect themselves from bad loans. Christine Dobby reports on the вҖңperfect stormвҖқ of debt problems. The aftermath: If banks are earning less, it could eventually become more difficult for Canadians to borrow money, an expert said. Go deeper: An April poll of about 1,500 Canadians found more than half were cutting back on non-essentials to cover their debt, while 43 per cent reported sacrificing on some essentials, and 26 per cent said they would consider taking on extra work. More: With delinquency rates for credit cards and car loans increasing, mortgage defaults could be next, a report warns. Canadian Press File Photo immigration This student canвҖҷt blame a consultant for submitting a bogus college admission letter, a tribunal hears Amritraj Singh Batth, 25, is one of several international students from India fighting to stay in Canada after applying for a student visa through the same agent. The uphill battle against deportation comes after their admission letters for post-secondary institutions were found to be bogus. Advocates have said that itвҖҷs the students who are victims, finding out only years after the fact that there was an issue with their documentation. But a Canada Border Services Agency told a tribunal Thursday that Batth could not be absolved of his responsibility by blaming an unscrupulous consultant. Nicholas Keung reports on the case and what awaits Batth if the finding is upheld. Context: Some of the international students in the investigation have been in Canada since 2017, completed their studies, received work permits and been on the path to permanent residence. Word from Batth: вҖңIf I would have gotten to know prior to coming to Canada, I would never have entered Canada вҖҰ I never intended to come to Canada illegally. I wanted to have proper legal documentation to come here,вҖқ he told the tribunal under cross examination. Dreamstime Photo climate crisis Ontario will give you cash to turn down your A/C Do you have a heat pump or a smart thermostat and central air conditioning? The Ontario government would like to turn down your A/C by a few degrees when the electrical grid is straining under peak demand вҖ” and theyвҖҷll give you $75 for the right to do it for you, Marco Chown Oved reports. HereвҖҷs why Energy Minister Todd Smith says the new program is вҖңa win for customers, a win for the climate and a win for the province.вҖқ More: вҖңTo be clear: participants are always in control and can opt-out by adjusting their thermostat without losing incentives,вҖқ Smith said. Word from the Green Party: The program is вҖңa feeble attempt to slap a bandaid on a crumbling energy system that has worsened thanks to this governmentвҖҷs total mismanagement,вҖқ party leader Mike Schreiner said in a statement, adding that вҖңother provinces and countries are moving full-speed ahead with investments in renewable energy.вҖқ Wait, what? Shortly after being elected in 2018, Doug FordвҖҷs government cancelled more than 700 renewable energy projects and numerous energy conservation programs after years of industry fleeing the province and electricity demand stagnating. WHAT ELSE David Johnston forgot something important in his report вҖ” and it could give the reassurance Canadians need. Do 102 names fit on a ballot?! HereвҖҷs everything you need to know about voting in the mayoral election. MГ©lanie JolyвҖҷs office is upset over Conservative MP Leslyn Lewis and controversial preacher Charles McVetyвҖҷs plan to host an Israeli minister. Mayoral candidates sparred over economic issues during the fourth debate in just two days. HereвҖҷs what you need to know. The trip was years in the making. This is what CanadaвҖҷs Black politicians learned when they visited their U.S. peers. Toronto Pride is mulling cuts to the festival after policing and insurance costs more than tripled. Brampton is the most expensive Ontario city for car insurance. HereвҖҷs why the average premium is now over $2,700. The вҖңmom typesвҖқ trend is silly but irresistible. Which type are you: silky, crunchy, almond or вҖҰ scrunchie? Did you give your child an old phone to play with? Officials say many of these devices are to blame for accidental 911 calls. Changing a personal brand can be a hard sell, as Elizabeth HolmesвҖҷs attempted makeover as a wholesome suburban mom shows. Freedom Mobile has unveiled its first nationwide plan since the Rogers-Shaw deal. From the best Cannes Film Festival in years, the StarвҖҷs critic picks the top 10 movies. POV Steve Russell/The Star Three mayoral debates in one day, six long hours and one surprising winner. CLOSE-UP Steve Russell/The Star NATHAN PHILLIPS SQUARE: Six-year-old Canadian Gabriel Duclayna smiles after his parents Kenneth Duclayna and Regine Obsequo are sworn in as Canadian Citizens on Thursday. They are among thirty-seven people from 11 different countries sworn in as new Canadian citizens on Toronto Newcomer Day, an annual event held in May. Thank you for reading. You can reach me and the First Up team at firstup@thestar.ca. Ashley will see you back here tomorrow.
